criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented `subscribe_progress` as an `@asynccontextmanager` in `src/forge/adapters/guardkit/progress_subscriber.py`. Verified via `TestModuleSurface::test_subscribe_progress_is_async_context_manager` (object exposes `__aenter__`/`__aexit__`) and `test_module_path` (canonical module path).
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented `subscribe_progress` as an `@asynccontextmanager` in `src/forge/adapters/guardkit/progress_subscriber.py`. Verified via `TestModuleSurface::test_subscribe_progress_is_async_context_manager` (object exposes `__aenter__`/`__aexit__`) and `test_module_path` (canonical module path).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `ProgressSink` retains the most recent event per `(build_id,
criteria_verification.evidence: ProgressSink stores per-key `collections.deque(maxlen=N)` so append() drops the oldest entry on full — exactly the 'most recent observable' guarantee. Verified by `TestProgressSinkRetention::test_back_pressure_drops_oldest` (5 events into a 3-slot sink — last 3 retained, oldest 2 evicted) plus `test_latest_returns_most_recent`.
criteria_verification.notes: ProgressSink stores per-key `collections.deque(maxlen=N)` so append() drops the oldest entry on full — exactly the 'most recent observable' guarantee. Verified by `TestProgressSinkRetention::test_back_pressure_drops_oldest` (5 events into a 3-slot sink — last 3 retained, oldest 2 evicted) plus `test_latest_returns_most_recent`.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unsubscribe runs on context-manager exit, including the exception
criteria_verification.evidence: The async context manager wraps `yield` in a `try/finally` and awaits `sub.unsubscribe()` in the finally block. A second try/except inside the finally swallows unsubscribe failures so cleanup never propagates. Verified by `TestUnsubscribeOnExit::test_unsubscribes_on_normal_exit`, `test_unsubscribes_on_exception_exit` (RuntimeError raised inside the body), and `test_unsubscribe_failure_does_not_propagate`.
criteria_verification.notes: The async context manager wraps `yield` in a `try/finally` and awaits `sub.unsubscribe()` in the finally block. A second try/except inside the finally swallows unsubscribe failures so cleanup never propagates. Verified by `TestUnsubscribeOnExit::test_unsubscribes_on_normal_exit`, `test_unsubscribes_on_exception_exit` (RuntimeError raised inside the body), and `test_unsubscribe_failure_does_not_propagate`.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: If the NATS client is `None` or unavailable, `subscribe_progress`
criteria_verification.evidence: Two no-op paths implemented: (a) `nats_client is None` records one `PROGRESS_STREAM_UNAVAILABLE` warning, logs, and yields; (b) `subscribe()` raising likewise records the warning and yields. Verified by `TestUnavailableClient::test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns` (asserts `inside is True`, exactly one warning with code `progress_stream_unavailable`) and `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop` (uses `BrokenNATSClient` raising `ConnectionError`).
criteria_verification.notes: Two no-op paths implemented: (a) `nats_client is None` records one `PROGRESS_STREAM_UNAVAILABLE` warning, logs, and yields; (b) `subscribe()` raising likewise records the warning and yields. Verified by `TestUnavailableClient::test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns` (asserts `inside is True`, exactly one warning with code `progress_stream_unavailable`) and `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop` (uses `BrokenNATSClient` raising `ConnectionError`).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Each subscription is scoped to one
criteria_verification.evidence: `subject_for(build_id, subcommand)` builds the exact subject; `subscribe_progress` only subscribes to that one subject. Verified by `TestSubjectScoping::test_subscribes_to_correct_subject` (asserts the subscribe topic literal) and `test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent` (two `async with` blocks for the same build_id but distinct subcommands; each sink slot receives only its own events).
criteria_verification.notes: `subject_for(build_id, subcommand)` builds the exact subject; `subscribe_progress` only subscribes to that one subject. Verified by `TestSubjectScoping::test_subscribes_to_correct_subject` (asserts the subscribe topic literal) and `test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent` (two `async with` blocks for the same build_id but distinct subcommands; each sink slot receives only its own events).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Two concurrent builds against the same repo get isolated sinks (no
criteria_verification.evidence: ProgressSink owns no class- or module-level state — each instance has its own `_buffers` and `_warnings`. Verified by `TestParallelBuilds::test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks`: two distinct sinks for two build_ids; events delivered on each subject only land in the corresponding sink, and querying the cross-build slot returns None.
criteria_verification.notes: ProgressSink owns no class- or module-level state — each instance has its own `_buffers` and `_warnings`. Verified by `TestParallelBuilds::test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks`: two distinct sinks for two build_ids; events delivered on each subject only land in the corresponding sink, and querying the cross-build slot returns None.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Invalid payloads (malformed JSON, missing fields) are dropped with a
criteria_verification.evidence: The inbound `_on_envelope` callback wraps `GuardKitProgressEvent.model_validate(envelope.payload)` in a try/except catching `ValidationError`, `ValueError`, `TypeError`; on failure it records a `PROGRESS_PAYLOAD_INVALID` warning and returns (never raises). Verified by `TestMalformedPayloads::test_missing_required_field_dropped`, `test_wrong_payload_type_dropped`, and `test_valid_event_after_malformed_still_recorded` (subscription continues consuming after a bad payload).
criteria_verification.notes: The inbound `_on_envelope` callback wraps `GuardKitProgressEvent.model_validate(envelope.payload)` in a try/except catching `ValidationError`, `ValueError`, `TypeError`; on failure it records a `PROGRESS_PAYLOAD_INVALID` warning and returns (never raises). Verified by `TestMalformedPayloads::test_missing_required_field_dropped`, `test_wrong_payload_type_dropped`, and `test_valid_event_after_malformed_still_recorded` (subscription continues consuming after a bad payload).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit tests with a fake NATS client: ordered delivery, back-pressure
criteria_verification.evidence: Hand-rolled `FakeNATSClient` and `BrokenNATSClient` (no nats-py imports). Coverage: ordered delivery (`test_ordered_delivery_within_one_subscription`), back-pressure (`test_back_pressure_drops_oldest`), malformed payload (3 cases under TestMalformedPayloads), unavailable client (`test_none_client_is_noop_and_warns`, `test_subscribe_failure_yields_noop`), parallel subscriptions (`test_parallel_subscriptions_are_independent`, `test_two_builds_have_isolated_sinks`). 22 tests, all passing.
